spec_match.rationale: Installs a real same-origin payment handler, calls `PaymentRequest.show()`, and branches on the actual `DOMException.name` — exactly the AbortError vs OperationError mechanism the spec defines.

interactive.rationale: Scenario buttons, install/canMakePayment controls, Pay button, and live code-trace panel all drive a real handler — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb promises a five-outcome live checkout with code trace; the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers success, abort, and three OperationError sub-messages — but doesn't show the legacy Chrome <149 behaviour where every failure collapsed to AbortError, and doesn't exercise the `NotSupportedError` branch its catch block mentions.

references.rationale: The see-also list links only to sibling concepts and the feature index — the canonical chromestatus.com/feature/5942637229113344 link is missing on this page (every demo page is supposed to carry it as the self-heal anchor).

design_system.rationale: Variables are used throughout but the page ships unicode glyphs (`✓`, `⚠`, `←`, `▶`) as UI icons; the project rule is inline SVG icons, never emoji, for all UI elements.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link to see-also
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must include https://chromestatus.com/feature/5942637229113344 — it is the recovery anchor for slug regressions. The see-also currently lists only siblings and the feature index. Add a `ChromeStatus entry` list item.

openQuestions.title: Replace unicode glyphs with inline SVG icons
openQuestions.detail: Result panels and the pay button use `✓ ⚠ ← ▶` characters; project convention forbids emoji/glyph icons in UI. Swap each for an inline SVG using `currentColor` so they respect the design system colour tokens.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the pre-149 collapse-to-AbortError baseline
openQuestions.detail: The headline of this feature is `now you can distinguish what previously collapsed`. Add a toggle that simulates the legacy behaviour (handler throws OperationError, but PaymentRequest still surfaces it as AbortError) so visitors can see the before/after on the same page.

openQuestions.title: Expose the `err.message` payload parsing
openQuestions.detail: The handler library uses a JSON-shaped `err.message` to convey card-expired / insufficient-funds detail. The trace prints the parsed message but never shows the raw JSON envelope vs the parsed object. A `show raw error` toggle would make the parsing contract explicit.

openQuestions.title: Cover NotSupportedError and other fall-through branches
openQuestions.detail: The catch block in the snippet mentions a generic error fall-through, but no scenario triggers it. Add a `PaymentRequest constructor rejects` scenario (e.g. no installed handler) so the third branch in the merchant logic is actually exercised in the demo.

summary: A polished end-to-end checkout that nails the spec match and interactivity, but ships unicode glyphs as icons (forbidden by the design rules) and forgets the chromestatus self-heal link. Both are mechanical fixes that should land together.
